texto: I believe that children in this country need a more robust literary diet than they are getting. …It does not hurt them to read about good and evil, love and hate, life and death. Nor do I think they should read only about things that they understand. '…a man’s reach should exceed his grasp.' So should a child’s. For myself, I will never talk down to, or draw down to, children.(from the author's acceptance speech for the Caldecott award)
explicacao: Orientação direta de Barbara Cooney sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. A afirmação “I believe that children in this country need a more robust literary diet than they are getting. ……” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a reflexao, child, children, útil em momentos de luto e children.
